_description: React-specific anti-patterns for AI coding agents — practitioner-informed rules from TkDodo, Kent C. Dodds, Dan Abramov, Nadia Makarevich, Mark Erikson, and Tanner Linsley

rules.message: [architecture] Direct DOM manipulation in React — use refs or state instead
[convention] Disabled exhaustive-deps — suppressing this rule causes stale closures and subtle bugs. Fix the dependency array or restructure the effect.
rules._source: TkDodo / Dominik Dorfmeister (tkdodo.eu), Sandor Farkas / Wolf-Tech (wolf-tech.io)

rules.message: [architecture] Object literal in Context Provider value — creates new reference every render, causing all consumers to re-render. Extract to useMemo or state.
[convention] Unnecessary cleanup — React Testing Library auto-runs cleanup since v9. Remove this call.
[testability] Direct DOM query in tests — use Testing Library queries (getByRole, getByLabelText) instead of container.querySelector
[architecture] Legacy Redux import — use @reduxjs/toolkit instead of raw redux for reducers, actions, and store setup
[architecture] Using array index as key — causes incorrect state preservation when list items reorder. Use a stable unique identifier.
[async-misuse] async function passed directly to useEffect — useEffect callbacks must be synchronous. Define the async function inside and call it.
[convention] Disabled rules-of-hooks — this rule prevents calling hooks conditionally or in loops. Never suppress it; restructure the component instead.
